title: Tefaun
content:

'''Tefauns''' were a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-skinned, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with ridged heads and bulging [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The Tefaun [[Banker/Legends|banker]] [[Kelek]] purchased [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] resort, launching a brutal [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competition called [[the Big Game]]. When the Wheel lost clientele and experienced reduced revenues, Kelek borrowed money from the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] to win back customers but was unable to meet her payment schedule, forcing her to sell her shares to businessman [[Dominic Raynor]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Soth Petikkin]] from [[Tefau]] served as a persuasive [[Recruitment Agent|recruiter]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], using his contacts in [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Palace]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to gain support for the Alliance.

==Biology and appearance==
The [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] Tefaun [[Species/Legends|species]] had two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and legs, a torso, a neck, and a head. One individual had [[Color/Legends|blue]] skin, bulging [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a ridged, disc-shaped head.<ref name="SWC 242" />

==Society and culture==
At least one Tefaun wore a [[Robe/Legends|robe]] as [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], along with a [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]].<ref name="SWC 242" />

==History==
[[Kelek]] was a Tefaun who lived during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. She became a [[Banker/Legends|banker]], purchased the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] resort known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]], and launched [[the Big Game]], a brutal [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competition that was held aboard the station. Kelek, who had by then also gained both the nicknames Kelek the Blue and Kelek the Insensate, was the Wheel's third owner following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of her predecessor, the [[J'feh/Legends|J'feh]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Skijid Vrescot]]. The large number of [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrels]] frequenting the facility deterred wealthy clientele from visiting the station, and the Wheel experienced reduced revenues. As a result, Kelek borrowed money from the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], hoping it would help regain lost customers. However, after Kelek became unable to meet her payment schedule, the Banking Clan called in her loan, and she was forced to sell her shares in the station to the businessman [[Dominic Raynor]].<ref name="Vegas">{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

Later, during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], [[Soth Petikkin]] was a [[Force-attunement|Force-attuned]] Tefaun who possessed limited [[Precognition/Legends|precognition]]. Native to [[Tefau]], he served as a persuasive [[Recruitment Agent|recruiter]]<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Soth Petikkin}}</ref> for the Alliance.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|22|Petikkin, Soth}}</ref> Petikkin used his contacts in<ref name="CCG" /> the crime lord<ref name="Jabba CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|130|Jabba the Hutt (Jabba Desilijic Tiure)}}</ref> [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt's]] [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|palace]]<ref name="CCG" /> on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]<ref name="Jabba CSWE" /> to find support for the Alliance.<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Tefaun species' origins date back to the production stages of the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. A number of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] maquettes were designed for possible inclusion in the film's Jabba's Palace scenes but were scrapped before the final film's release. Among them was a robed biped known only as "Alien 25," who might have been a candidate for one of Jabba's [[Aide/Legends|aides]].<ref name="SWC 242" /> In [[1998]], the character Soth Petikkin was created on a [http://www.starwarsccg.org/cardlists/images/starwars/SpecialEdition-Light/large/sothpetikkin.gif card] for the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]{{'s}} ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' set, which used a manipulated image of the "Alien 25" maquette and brought the species into [[canon]].<ref name="CCG" />

In [[September]] [[2013]], the species was first named as "Tefaun" when the Kelek character was created for the [[Star Wars Blog]] article "[[Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel]]" on [[StarWars.com]].<ref name="Vegas" /> [[Abel G. Peña]], one of the article's authors, confirmed on his Facebook page that the Tefauns were indeed the same as Soth Petikkin's species.<ref name="Pena Facebook">{{Facebook|username=abelgpena|url=abelgpena/posts/10202130456368996?comment_id=7172944&offset=0&total_comments=2|name=Abel G. Peña|date=October 8, 2013|quote=That's absolutely right [that Soth Petikkin's race is the "Tefaun" species], Alban.|archiveurl=http://img95.xooimage.com/files/d/3/f/tefaun-415de8d.png}}</ref>
